raw_consumer_statement: I was contacted on Tuesday XXXX XX/XX/year> by Citibank saying they though a charge of {$1100.00} was fraud. I was grateful for their vigilance, and asked about the dispute resolution. I was routed to XXXX different off-shore call centers and finally told that the fraud department would not take a look at the charge as yet, because the vendor had not responded. I don't understand that a charge of that amount would remain on my record because of the vendor behavior. We all agree that the charge is a fraud, so why is this policy so dilatory?
